assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and has created a recognised intragroup receivable/payable.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a way that creates foreign currency risk between group entities with different functional currencies, and the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a monetary asset or liability, and IFRS 9 6.3.6 allows its foreign currency risk to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign-currency settlement cash flows
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The same foreign currency exposure on settlement of the recognised intragroup monetary item can also be evaluated as variability in cash flows affecting profit or loss, making cash flow hedge designation a genuine candidate route.
